pathways: Phytochrome signaling (phyA far-red, phyB/C/D/E red, COP1/SPA degradation, HY5 stabilization, PIF phosphorylation/degradation)
UV-B signaling (UVR8 monomerization → COP1 → HY5/HYH; RUP1/RUP2 negative feedback)
Phototropin signaling (PHOT1/PHOT2 — minor for hypocotyl elongation, primarily phototropism)
Brassinosteroid (BRI1/BAK1, BIN2 GSK3, BZR1/BES1; biosynthesis: DET2, DWF4, CPD; catabolism: BAS1/CYP734A1, SOB7/CYP72C1)
Gibberellin (biosynthesis GA20ox/GA3ox, catabolism GA2ox; GID1A/B/C receptor; DELLA proteins RGA, GAI, RGL1-3; SCF^SLY1 degradation)
Auxin (YUC/TAA1 biosynthesis, GH3 conjugation, TIR1/AFB receptors, Aux/IAA repressors, ARF6/7/8 TFs, PIN efflux carriers)
Ethylene (triple response in dark): ETR receptors, CTR1, EIN2, EIN3/EIL1, ERF1, EBF1/2 negative feedback
Temperature / thermomorphogenesis: PIF4 master regulator; ELF3 (Evening Complex), HSFA1d, RCB-HMR, TCP5/13/17, phyB thermal reversion, PIF4→YUC8/SAUR auxin amplification
Shade avoidance (low R:FR): PIF7 nuclear accumulation, HFR1 antagonist, PAR1/PAR2, ATHB2/ATHB4/HAT1/HAT2/HAT3 HD-Zip II family, BBX24
Circadian clock control: CCA1, LHY, TOC1, PRR5/7/9, Evening Complex (ELF3/ELF4/LUX), GI; gating of PIF activity
ABA (stress antagonism of elongation): ABA represses GA biosynthesis, stabilizes DELLA, represses YUC, represses PIF activity
hubs: BBX21, BBX22, BBX24, BBX25, BBX11 (HY5 cofactors)
canonical_mutants: phyA, phyB, phyA phyB, cry1, cry2, cry1 cry2, uvr8, phot1 phot2
cop1 (constitutively photomorphogenic in dark), spaQ (spa1;2;3;4), hy5, hyh, hy5 hyh, det1, det2, cop10
pif1, pif3, pif4, pif5, pif7, pifQ (pif1;3;4;5); PIF OE
bri1, det2, dwf4, cpd, bin2-1 (dominant GOF), bzr1-1D, bes1-D; BL treatment, brassinazole (Brz)
ga1, ga2, ga3, ga20ox, della pentuple (gai rga rgl1 rgl2 rgl3), sly1, gid1a gid1b gid1c; GA3, paclobutrazol (PAC)
yuc2 yuc5 yuc8 yuc9, tir1 afb2 afb3, arf6 arf8; IAA/NAA/picloram, NPA
etr1, ein2, ein3, ctr1; ACC, AVG
WT + low R:FR vs high R:FR; hfr1, pif7
WT 22 vs 28°C; pif4 at warm, elf3 at warm, xbat31
cca1 lhy, toc1, elf3, lux, gi
environmental_inputs: Light: continuous white, red (R), far-red (FR), blue (B), UV-B, dark
crosstalk: phyB ↔ BIN2 (light modulation of BR signaling)
HY5 ↔ BBX module (BBX11/21/22 positive, BBX24/25 negative)
PIF4 → YUC8 / SAUR (auxin amplification in thermomorphogenesis)
COP1 degrades both HY5 AND BBX21 (dark-integrator)
ABA → DELLA stabilization → PIF inhibition → reduced YUC → reduced auxin (ABA-GA-auxin axis)

gaps.biological_justification: HY5 is the master photomorphogenic TF with hundreds of ChIP-seq targets. Step 1 captured COP1/SPA-mediated degradation but missed the BBX11 positive feedback loop and most direct downstream targets (BBX22, RUP1/RUP2).
search_queries: HY5 ChIP-seq direct targets BBX22 HFR1 LAF1 photomorphogenesis Arabidopsis
perturbations_added: BBX11 KO/OE, BBX11_BBX21 double, BBX25 KO/OE, BBX24_BBX25 double

gaps.pathway: phyA signaling (FHY1, FHL, FHY3, FAR1, LAF1)
gaps.biological_justification: phyA is the canonical far-red photoreceptor. Step 1 captured phyA→Hypocotyl but completely missed the FHY1/FHL nuclear-import adapters, the FHY3/FAR1 transcription factors that drive FHY1/FHL expression, and downstream LAF1/HFR1 effector TFs. Without these, BUILDER cannot model far-red HIR signaling.
search_queries: FHY1 FHL FHY3 FAR1 phyA hypocotyl Arabidopsis nuclear import
edges_added: phyA↔FHY1, FHY1→phyA nuclear import, FHY3/FAR1→FHY1/FHL transcription, FHY1/FHL→LAF1/HFR1 complex assembly, LAF1→Hypocotyl
perturbations_added: fhy1, fhy1 FHL RNAi, fhy3, far1, hfr1, LAF1 RNAi

gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 captured UVR8→COP1→HY5 forward signaling but missed the canonical negative feedback loop: UV-B/UVR8/HY5 induce RUP1/RUP2 transcription, which directly bind UVR8 to drive its redimerization and terminate signaling. rup1 rup2 double is hypersensitive to UV-B.
search_queries: RUP1 RUP2 UVR8 UV-B negative feedback Arabidopsis hypocotyl
perturbations_added: rup1 rup2 double KO, RUP2 OE, rup2 single KO

gaps.pathway: Shade avoidance HD-Zip II module (ATHB2, ATHB4, HAT1/2/3)
gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had PIF7-shade and HFR1/PAR1 antagonism but completely missed the HD-Zip II shade transcription factors (ATHB2, ATHB4, HAT1, HAT2, HAT3) that are direct PIF targets and major shade-induced elongation effectors. Also missed PIN3/PIN4/PIN7 auxin transport for shade.
search_queries: ATHB2 ATHB4 PAR2 shade avoidance Arabidopsis hypocotyl HD-ZIP
perturbations_added: athb2 KO/OE, athb4 OE, hat3 athb4 double, pin3 pin4 pin7 triple

gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had a generic GID1→DELLA edge but the three Arabidopsis GA receptors GID1A/B/C were absent as individual nodes. The triple gid1a gid1b gid1c is a canonical extreme-dwarf GA-insensitive mutant; individual receptors have non-redundant roles (GID1A primary for stem; GID1B highest DELLA affinity).
search_queries: GID1a GID1b GID1c Arabidopsis triple mutant DELLA hypocotyl receptor
perturbations_added: gid1a gid1c double, gid1a gid1b double, gid1b gid1c double

gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 captured BR biosynthesis (DET2, DWF4, CPD) and signaling but BAS1/CYP734A1 had only 1 generic edge and SOB7/CYP72C1 was absent. These two P450 catabolic enzymes are tightly coupled to phyB signaling (overexpression rescues phyB-4 long hypocotyl).
search_queries: SOB7 BAS1 CYP734A1 CYP72B1 brassinosteroid catabolism Arabidopsis hypocotyl
perturbations_added: bas1 KO/OE, sob7 KO, bas1 sob7 double

gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had the equivalent quadruple under the gene-list label PIF1_PIF3_PIF4_PIF5 but lacked the canonical 'pifQ' shorthand needed for cross-referencing. Phototropin and chemical treatment perturbations also absent.
search_queries: pifQ pif1 pif3 pif4 pif5 quadruple Arabidopsis dark photomorphogenic

gaps.biological_justification: ABA is a well-documented elongation inhibitor that acts via reduced GA biosynthesis (GA3ox/GA20ox down, GA2ox up), DELLA stabilization, PIF repression, and reduced YUC auxin biosynthesis. Step 1 had no ABA edges at all.
search_queries: ABA hypocotyl ABI3 ABI5 Arabidopsis elongation
edges_added: ABA→GA3ox1/GA20ox1 (negative), ABA→GA2ox1 (positive), ABA→DELLA stabilization, ABA→YUC3, ABA→Hypocotyl

gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had BBX21/BBX22/BBX24 as Hypocotyl edges but the BBX25 negative regulator and BBX11 positive feedback hub were absent. BBX25 forms inactive heterodimer with HY5 to repress photomorphogenesis; BBX11 directly activates HY5 forming a positive feedback loop.
search_queries: BBX25 HY5 BBX22 BBX11 photomorphogenesis Arabidopsis

gaps.biological_justification: TCP5/13/17 are PIF4 transcriptional cofactors required for thermomorphogenesis. CRY1 sequesters TCP17 at low temperature; warm temperature releases TCP17 to enhance PIF4 activity at YUC8/IAA19. This crosstalk loop was entirely missing from Step 1.
search_queries: TCP17 PIF4 thermomorphogenesis Arabidopsis hypocotyl temperature
perturbations_added: tcp5 tcp13 tcp17 triple, TCP5/TCP13/TCP17 OE, tcp17 single

gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had these 5 nodes with 3-5 outgoing edges each but zero upstream regulators. DET1's CDD complex partners (COP10, DDB1A/B), SPA1 transcriptional regulation, and clock interconnections (CCA1/LHY→PRR5) were missing.
search_queries: CCA1 LHY PRR5 PRR7 PRR9 morning loop transcription Arabidopsis
edges_added: CCA1→PRR5/PRR7/PRR9, LHY→PRR5/PRR7/PRR9 (added in B5 gap-fill batch during Step 1)
gaps.unclosable_reason: DET1, PAR1, SPA1 still source-only after merge — these are constitutively expressed regulators with sparse promoter-level regulation in literature; documented as residual gaps for BUILDER.

gaps.node: PMC3489987 (phyB inhibits PIF binding paper) extracted only 1 edge in Step 1
gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 read this paper full-text but extracted only 1 edge. The paper has 5+ edges in its Results section (Pfr-phyB binds PIF1/PIF3, sequesters PIF1 from target, PIF3→PIL1 repression, etc.). Most of these edges were captured from sister papers (PMC2276449, PMC6048787) so this is only a documentation issue, not a coverage gap.
gaps.unclosable_reason: Same edges captured from related papers; no new biology missed.

gaps.description: PIN1/PIN3 auxin transport in hypocotyl elongation
gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had no PIN auxin efflux carriers. PIN3 is canonical for shade-induced lateral auxin redistribution. Partially closed via shade pathway paper (PMC6313891) which adds PIN3_PIN4_PIN7 triple perturbation, but PIN→Hypocotyl edges per individual carrier remain implicit.
perturbations_added: pin3 pin4 pin7 triple (from G004 paper)
gaps.unclosable_reason: Individual PIN1/PIN3 → Hypocotyl edges not extracted; relies on broader shade-paper coverage. Documented for BUILDER as a residual.

bidirectional_audit.interpretation: Source-only count rose from 7 to 11 because the merge introduced new nodes (FHY3, FAR1, ABA, GA4_hormone) whose own upstream regulators were not curated in this round. Light and Temperature are environmental inputs and correctly remain source-only. DET1/SPA1/PAR1/PRR5/LHY remain genuine bidirectional gaps (G006). The new ABA node should ideally have NCED/ABA biosynthesis upstream — flagged for BUILDER.

extraction_density_audit.comment: Step 1 extraction density was already excellent (9.42 edges/paper). Only one paper (PMC3489987 / 10.1111/j.1365-313X.2012.05114.x) extracted 1 edge, but the same biology was independently captured from sibling papers PMC2276449 and PMC6048787, so re-reading was not necessary. New papers (10) added in second round averaged ~7.4 edges/paper.

residual_gaps_for_builder: DET1 has 6 outgoing edges but 0 incoming — its regulation by COP10/DDB1A/B in the CDD ubiquitin-ligase complex was not curated; treat DET1 as constitutively-expressed source node.
SPA1 has 4 outgoing edges but 0 incoming — SPA1 is mostly post-translationally regulated (CRY1/CRY2 binding); no transcriptional upstream curated; flag as `literature_gap` source node.
PAR1 has 4 outgoing edges but 0 incoming — PIF-mediated activation is mentioned in shade reviews but no specific Arabidopsis paper has clean PIF→PAR1 edge in the curated set.
ABA hormone has 6 outgoing edges but 0 incoming — NCED biosynthesis enzymes not curated for hypocotyl context; treat ABA as input/source.

GA4_hormone has 4 outgoing but the upstream is the GA20ox/GA3ox enzymes (separate nodes); BUILDER should include GA biosynthesis chain GA20ox1→GA4_hormone→GID1→DELLA degradation rather than treating GA4_hormone as source.

PIN1/PIN3 individual auxin efflux carriers not curated as separate nodes; only pin3 pin4 pin7 triple perturbation captured — shade-induced lateral auxin redistribution mechanism is implicit in PIF→YUC edges.

Phototropin (PHOT1/PHOT2) signaling minimally captured (one perturbation only) — phototropins primarily regulate phototropism/stomatal opening, not bulk hypocotyl elongation, so this is a low-priority gap.
FHY3/FAR1 themselves lack curated upstream regulators — they autoregulate and are positively regulated by HY5, but those autoregulatory loops were not extracted in this round.
BR biosynthesis missing the full enzymatic chain: only DWF4/CPD/DET2/BAS1/SOB7 captured; ROT3/CYP90C1, BR6ox1/CYP85A1 absent.

id_provenance.preservation_check: All Step 1 IDs verified present in merged files (E001-E244 ⊂ merged edge IDs; T001-T123 ⊂ merged test IDs).
